items.text: Dana Whitfield, product manager at a healthcare data company in Portland
Sam, partner of six years; described as 'great' support during difficult times
Marcus, brother, lives in Denver; they are close but haven't seen each other in over a year, currently only phone contact
Mid-quarter at work with roadmap review on Thursday 2025-02-06; working through prioritization between interoperability work and analytics dashboard for exec team
items.archivedReason: Work roadmap review from 2025-02-06 is stale; nearly three months old with no follow-up

items.text: Wants to see brother Marcus in person this year after over a year of phone-only contact — described as 'a real intention'

supersededBy.text: Upgraded to concrete plan after Nina's birth 2025-03-06; see new thread about visiting before summer 2025 (resolved 2025-03-09)

items.text: Lost mother recently (as of 2025-02-03 session, described as 'heavy winter'); said 'it colors everything'; did not want to discuss details in this session
Priya, closest friend from grad school; ceramicist and freelance designer; they signed up for a Tuesday night pottery class together starting 2025-02-12
[as of 2025-02-12] Attends Tuesday pottery class with Priya; first standing regular activity together in years
items.compressedFrom: Started attending Tuesday night pottery class with friend Priya (as of 2025-02-12); first standing regular activity they've had together in years

items.text: Mother had a 22-foot wooden sailboat called the Dorothy Jean, named after Dana's grandmother. Mom taught Dana to sail on that boat. The boat has been under a tarp in Priya's dad's barn near Scappoose since mom got sick. On Sunday 2025-02-09, Dana pulled the tarp off and sanded one plank — the first step in deciding to restore the boat slowly, with no deadline. Dana said working with hands on something of mom's 'felt like the first right thing in months.' (2025-02-12)
Restoring mother's sailboat Dorothy Jean at barn near Scappoose; working Sundays, slowly, no deadline (started 2025-02-09)

Training for Lakefront Marathon October 2025; signed up 2025-02-24. Running four times weekly, long run Saturdays (eight miles 2025-02-22 along river). Casual runner for years (5Ks, one half marathon ~four years ago). Chose marathon as 'something with a long arc' during difficult winter.
items.compressedFrom: Training for Lakefront Marathon in October 2025; signed up 2025-02-24. Running four times per week with long run every Saturday morning. Did eight miles on Saturday 2025-02-22 along the river. Has been a casual runner for years (5Ks, one half marathon about four years ago). Chose marathon as 'something with a long arc' that's just theirs during difficult winter.

items.text: On 2025-02-24, Dana said of signing up for the marathon: 'It's the first time since mom that I've been excited about something in the future instead of dreading it.'
Elena: Marcus's partner; gave birth to daughter Nina 2025-03-06
items.compressedFrom: Elena: Elena, Marcus's partner; gave birth to daughter Nina on 2025-03-06 (as of 2025-03-09)

items.text: Nina: niece, daughter of Marcus and Elena; born 2025-03-06. Marcus called Dana from hospital ~2am to share news. Dana 'cried at my desk' seeing first photo of Nina in knit hat.
items.compressedFrom: Nina: Nina, niece, daughter of Marcus and Elena; born 2025-03-06. Marcus called Dana from hospital at ~2am to share the news. Dana said 'I cried at my desk' seeing first photo of Nina in a knit hat. (as of 2025-03-09)

items.text: On 2025-03-09, Dana learned Marcus and Elena had their daughter Nina on 2025-03-06. Marcus called from the hospital hallway at ~2am 'just to say "she's here."' Dana said: 'We haven't talked like that — voice shaking, no agenda — in years. Denver suddenly feels way too far away.' (2025-03-09)

Wants to visit Marcus, Elena, and baby Nina in Denver before summer 2025 'while she's still tiny'; Marcus said 'the guest room is yours.' Dana described this as 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it' — 'the good kind' of obligation. (opened 2025-03-09)

supersededBy.text: Marcus flying to Portland with four-month-old Nina for Dorothy Jean's first sail weekend of July 12, 2025. Marcus said 'Mom would have required my attendance' and booked flights immediately. Dana described this as 'both debts, one weekend' — meeting Nina and having Marcus present for the boat launch.

items.text: [as of 2025-03-09] Marathon training on schedule; ran ten miles on Saturday 2025-03-07 in light snow flurries
[as of 2025-03-09] Dorothy Jean restoration progressing; port side fully sanded as of 2025-03-09

supersededBy.text: Dorothy Jean restoration progressing; starboard side sanded, started re-caulking hull seams (as of 2025-05-11)

items.text: On March 14, 2025, Dana scattered Mom's ashes at Beacon Rock in the Columbia River Gorge. Dana went alone. Beacon Rock was 'ours' — Mom used to take Dana there as a kid, and on the way up taught Dana to read cloud shapes: 'Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water.' Dana said: 'I stood at the top and the sky was doing exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved, and I let her go into the wind.' The urn had been in Dana's closet for months; Dana 'couldn't even look at that side of the closet.' Dana picked up a fir cone from the trail on the way down; it's on the windowsill above the kitchen sink now. Sam had soup waiting when Dana got home. Dana slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)
[as of 2025-03-18] In a cutback week in marathon training (as of 2025-03-18)

[as of 2025-04-01] Sam received Perception team lead offer at Austin robotics startup; strong offer in dream problem space, decision deadline early May 2025. Sam said he won't take it if it costs Dana this year of healing. Both weighing carefully.
items.compressedFrom: Sam received job offer as Perception team lead at robotics startup in Austin, Texas (as of 2025-04-01); strong offer in his dream problem space. Decision deadline early May 2025. Sam said he won't take it if it costs Dana this year of healing. Both weighing carefully, not fighting.

items.text: Deciding whether to move to Austin for Sam's job vs. staying in Portland. Could find PM work in Austin, but 'whole life fabric is here' — Priya, pottery, barn, boat, geography ('Mom's here... the Gorge, Beacon Rock, the barn. This geography is load-bearing for me right now'). Decision needed early May 2025.
items.compressedFrom: Deciding whether to move to Austin for Sam's job offer vs. staying in Portland. Dana feels torn: could find PM work in Austin, but 'whole life fabric is here' — Priya, pottery class, the barn, the boat restoration, and the geography itself ('Mom's here... the Gorge, Beacon Rock, the barn. This geography is load-bearing for me right now'). Decision needed by early May 2025. (opened 2025-04-01)

supersededBy.text: Sam accepted Austin robotics startup job with remote-from-Portland arrangement negotiated; starts June 2025, will work from spare room with week in Austin every couple months. Startup agreed to counter-offer. Dana and Sam staying in Portland. Decision made early May 2025 without conflict. Dana described relief at not having to leave 'load-bearing geography' this year.

items.text: Marathon training week six as of 2025-04-01; ran twelve miles on Saturday 2025-03-29 with no complaints

supersededBy.text: Withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025 on 2025-04-14 after ankle sprain; cannot safely complete training with eight-to-ten week recovery window. Withdrew registration immediately to avoid 'waffling.'

items.text: [as of 2025-04-14] Sprained ankle badly 2025-04-13 during Forest Park trail run (mile nine, wet root); grade-two or worse per urgent care. In walking boot; seeing orthopedist 2025-04-18. Eight to ten weeks before running possible.
items.compressedFrom: Sprained ankle badly on Saturday 2025-04-13 during trail run in Forest Park (mile nine, wet root); grade-two sprain or worse per urgent care. In walking boot; seeing orthopedist Thursday 2025-04-18. Doctor said eight to ten weeks before running possible again.

items.text: [as of 2025-04-14] Withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025 after ankle sprain; cannot safely complete training with eight-to-ten week recovery. Withdrew immediately to avoid 'waffling.'
items.compressedFrom: Withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025 on 2025-04-14 after ankle sprain; cannot safely complete training with eight-to-ten week recovery window. Withdrew registration immediately to avoid 'waffling.'

items.text: On 2025-04-14, after withdrawing from the marathon, Dana said: 'That training arc was the first future-shaped thing I'd built since mom.' Sam pointed out the fitness isn't lost and there's always next year; Dana said 'he's right, and I don't want to hear it yet.'
Ankle healing on schedule per orthopedist; walking boot comes off Thursday 2025-05-01, physical therapy to start after (as of 2025-04-27)

supersededBy.text: Ankle boot removed; walking normally-ish; attending physical therapy twice weekly (as of 2025-05-11)

items.text: Sam's decision deadline on Austin job offer is one week from 2025-04-27 (early May 2025 as previously stated)

supersededBy.text: Sam accepted Austin robotics startup Perception team lead role with remote-from-Portland arrangement; starts June 2025, working from spare room with week in Austin every couple months (as of 2025-05-11)

items.text: Rift with Priya after Dana declined to lend her $8,000 for ceramics studio lease deposit and buildout (as of ~2025-04-13 to 2025-04-15). Dana said no immediately and 'badly,' citing uncertain finances with Austin decision pending and past experience lending money to an ex that 'poisoned everything.' Priya went 'very quiet and then very polite.' They have skipped pottery class twice in the nearly two weeks since. Dana described it as delivering 'ten years of friendship a verdict in four seconds.' Dana is 'pretty sure' she won't change the answer but regrets how she delivered it. Described as 'too raw' to repair yet as of 2025-04-27. (opened 2025-04-27)

supersededBy.text: Reconciled with Priya on 2025-06-06 over coffee after Dana texted 'I miss you, can I buy you coffee, I want to talk about how I said what I said.' Three-hour conversation; both apologized (Dana for 'four-second verdict' delivery, Priya for going 'polite-and-frozen' instead of expressing hurt). Dana found regional arts infrastructure grant matching Priya's studio needs; spent Sunday 2025-06-09 helping write application (due end of June 2025). Dana said doing that together 'did more repair than any amount of talking about the fight.' Resumed pottery class Tuesday 2025-06-10; Priya mocked Dana's new bowl, which Dana took as sign they were 'really okay.'

items.text: [as of 2025-05-11] Sam accepted Austin robotics Perception lead role with remote-from-Portland arrangement; starts June 2025, working from spare room with week in Austin every couple months

supersededBy.text: Cleared by physical therapy for light jogging as of 2025-05-24; running twenty minutes on flat ground every other day. First run 2025-05-24 morning along esplanade. Still attending physical therapy.

supersededBy.text: Dorothy Jean restoration nearly complete; hull caulked and painted as of 2025-06-08. Described as 'close' to water-ready, 'actually-in-the-water-this-summer close.' Dana said she keeps 'standing back and looking at her and getting a lump in my throat.'

items.text: On 2025-05-11, after Sam negotiated remote arrangement for Austin job and they decided to stay in Portland, Dana said: 'When he told me they'd agreed, I cried a little, which surprised me. I hadn't let myself feel how scared I was of leaving this year — the barn, the boat, Beacon Rock, all of it. And I'd been quietly rehearsing how to be graceful about going. Not needing the rehearsal anymore was the relief.' (2025-05-11)
Cleared by physical therapy for light jogging as of 2025-05-24; running twenty minutes on flat ground every other day. First run 2025-05-24 morning along esplanade. Still attending physical therapy.

supersededBy.text: Running thirty minutes on flat ground every other day (as of 2025-06-08); progressing from twenty-minute runs cleared in late May 2025. Still in post-ankle-sprain recovery.

items.text: Sam built out spare room for remote work setup (three monitors, foam panels); starts Austin robotics job in approximately two weeks from 2025-05-24 (early June 2025). Described as 'nervous-excited' about start.
items.archivedReason: Sam's job start (June 2025) and spare room setup are now historical context; cs-30 captures the ongoing remote arrangement, which is the durable fact.

items.text: Has made peace with not running Lakefront Marathon October 2025; sees no responsible training path from twenty-minute jogs in late May to 26.2 miles in October. Open to signing up again next year. Current goal: run pain-free by fall without re-injury. Described learning 'something about not white-knuckling timelines' from the ankle injury experience.
Running thirty minutes on flat ground every other day (as of 2025-06-08); progressing from twenty-minute runs cleared in late May 2025. Still in post-ankle-sprain recovery.

supersededBy.text: Dorothy Jean restoration complete; marine survey confirms soundness. Scheduled for launch weekend of July 12, 2025 at Cathedral Park boat ramp. Priya's dad lending truck and trailer for transport. (as of 2025-06-21)

items.text: On 2025-06-08, describing the nearly-complete Dorothy Jean restoration, Dana said: 'I keep standing back and looking at her and getting a lump in my throat.' When told 'Your mom's boat, nearly ready to sail again. That's not nothing,' Dana replied: 'It's very much not nothing.' (2025-06-08)

Dorothy Jean restoration complete; marine survey confirms soundness. Scheduled for launch weekend of July 12, 2025 at Cathedral Park boat ramp. Priya's dad lending truck and trailer for transport. (as of 2025-06-21)
items.archivedReason: Dorothy Jean launch (July 12, 2025) is now past; sal-44 captures the emotionally significant outcome. The boat being water-ready is no longer 'current state' but accomplished history.

items.text: On 2025-06-21, Dana set the date for Dorothy Jean's first sail: weekend of July 12, 2025 at Cathedral Park. Marcus is flying out with four-month-old Nina (who will be four months old). Marcus said 'Mom would have required my attendance' and booked flights that night. Dana said: 'Nina will be four months old. She obviously won't remember it, but there's going to be a photo of her in a tiny life jacket on our mother's boat, and I need that photo to exist.' Dana plans to check the sky that morning the way Mom taught her: 'If it's a mare's tail sky I'm taking it as a blessing.' Sam will be aboard; has been 'reading knots tutorials like there's an exam.' Plan is 'just a gentle afternoon on the river, nothing heroic.' Dana said: 'The point isn't the sailing... The point is who's aboard, and whose boat it is.' (2025-06-21)
Running forty minutes on flat ground every other day (as of 2025-06-21); progressing from thirty-minute runs in early June 2025. Still in post-ankle-sprain recovery.

supersededBy.text: Running forty-five minutes on flat ground regularly (as of 2025-09-06); progressing from forty-minute runs in June 2025. Post-ankle-sprain recovery continuing.

items.text: Priya's regional arts infrastructure grant application submitted on time (end of June 2025); Dana helped write it on Sunday 2025-06-09 (as of 2025-06-21)
items.archivedReason: Priya's grant application submission (June 2025) is complete historical context; no ongoing thread depends on tracking this as current state.

items.text: On July 12, 2025, the Dorothy Jean sailed for the first time since restoration. Dana, Sam, Marcus, and four-month-old Nina aboard for a gentle afternoon on the Columbia River. The sky was 'high thin stuff, not quite a mare's tail but close,' and Dana decided that counted as a blessing. There is a photo of Nina asleep in a tiny life jacket against Marcus's chest with the mainsail up behind them. Dana said: 'I have already had it printed twice.' (2025-07-19)

[as of 2025-07-19] Shipped interoperability platform in mid-July 2025 after eighteen months of development and internal politics; CEO gave public recognition in all-hands meeting. Major professional milestone.

On 2025-07-19, reflecting on the interoperability platform launch (mid-July 2025), Dana said: 'I stood there in the launch channel with all the confetti emojis and felt... nothing. Not modest-nothing. Actual, echoing nothing.' Dana described this as rattling: 'I hit the biggest professional moment of my life and it was like biting into wax fruit.' Dana said: 'For fifteen years I have basically been the person who is her work. Not in a grim way — I mean it like an engine. The next launch, the next rung, the harder problem. That's how I've always known I'm okay. It's how I know who I am. I'm the one who never turns down the bigger role. Ambition's been my whole spine.' Dana attributed the numbness to grief: 'Everything's been a half-step muted since Mom in a way that still ambushes me.' (2025-07-19)

Experiencing high work stress post-interoperability platform launch (mid-July 2025); production incident with exec demanding constant availability. Described work as 'pressure cooker' and 'having me by the throat' (as of 2025-08-02)

supersededBy.text: Work production incident from post-interoperability platform launch (mid-July 2025) finally closed as of 2025-09-06; work described as 'still a grind' but incident pressure resolved. (as of 2025-09-06)

items.text: [as of 2025-08-16] VP Elaine offered Dana director role (head of merged product org) 2025-08-15; Dana declined for 2025, citing platform stabilization needs and not wanting to transition org while still escalation point. Elaine accepted; door open for spring 2026. Dana described as 'defensible not now, not a no.'
items.compressedFrom: VP Elaine offered Dana director role (head of merged product org, roughly doubled org chart) on Thursday 2025-08-15; Dana declined for 2025, citing interoperability platform stabilization needs and not wanting to transition org leadership while still escalation point for production incidents. Elaine accepted reasoning; door remains open for spring 2026. Dana described this as 'defensible not now, not a no.' (as of 2025-08-16)

items.text: Enrolled in twelve-week Saturday intensive ceramics class (wheel throwing) at studio in Southeast Portland; started first Saturday of September 2025. Three hours every Saturday morning with 'a little scary and very good' teacher. Described as 'the first thing in months that felt like me' and 'the loudest my own head has been quiet since Beacon Rock.' Continues Tuesday pottery class with Priya separately. (as of 2025-09-06)
Running forty-five minutes on flat ground regularly (as of 2025-09-06); progressing from forty-minute runs in June 2025. Post-ankle-sprain recovery continuing.

Work production incident from post-interoperability platform launch (mid-July 2025) finally closed as of 2025-09-06; work described as 'still a grind' but incident pressure resolved. (as of 2025-09-06)

On 2025-09-06, describing the new Saturday ceramics intensive, Dana said: 'I've been circling it since the boat, honestly. The barn work, the sanding, the caulking — I noticed that the hours I spent with my hands on the Dorothy Jean were the only hours all spring where my brain went quiet. Really quiet. And when the boat launched I felt this little panic of "okay, but now what do I do with my hands." So: clay.' Dana said the class 'felt like me. Not like a thing I'm supposed to want, or a box I'm checking. Like me. Three hours where nobody needs an escalation decision and the clay does not care that I'm a product manager. It was the loudest my own head has been quiet since Beacon Rock.' (2025-09-06)

Priya received arts infrastructure grant for ceramics studio buildout (September 2025); signing lease next month. Priya offered Dana a shelf in the new studio for Dana's work; Dana accepted. Grant application was one Dana helped write in June 2025.
Nina (niece) is crawling as of September 2025; Marcus sends frequent videos
Sam traveling to Austin next week (from 2025-09-20) for regular in-person stint with robotics startup team; remote arrangement working well, team reception positive
Dorothy Jean sailed twice more over summer 2025 after July 12 launch; described as 'lovely quiet summer' on the water with Sam
Summoned for jury duty in October 2025 (as of 2025-09-20)

On 2025-09-20, after learning Priya received the arts infrastructure grant for her ceramics studio, Dana said: 'I don't think I've ever been so happy about something that has nothing to do with my own life.' Priya called Dana crying, then made fun of Dana for crying; Dana described this as 'our whole friendship in one phone call.' Dana said getting to feel like a good friend again 'after the four-second-verdict spring is medicine.' (2025-09-20)
Q4 planning described as 'bloodbath'; staying online until eleven most nights as of early October 2025. Work stress causing interpersonal strain.

Reduced to four-day work week (Fridays off) effective 2025-11-01; twenty percent pay cut. HR processed 2025-10-24. Told VP Elaine it was to 'protect stabilization bandwidth'; Elaine understood as work optimization.

Restoring Priya's dad's barn near Scappoose (the barn where Dorothy Jean was stored); working Fridays starting November 2025. Re-siding north wall, fixing roofline, building workbench. Barn described as 'falling down' and Priya's dad 'too old to fix it.' This is where Dana 'found my hands again this spring.'
Moving Mom's garden tools from Dana's basement back to the barn near Scappoose where they belong (as of late October 2025). Also tending Mom's roses at the old house before it sells.

On 2025-10-25, explaining the decision to go four days a week, Dana said: 'The real reason is that the days I spend at the wheel or in that barn are the days I feel like a person, and the days I spend making roadmap tradeoffs are the days I feel like a very competent function. And I'm forty-one and I lost my mom this year and I'm no longer willing to spend five out of seven days being a function. That's the real reason. It's not burnout — the work's fine, I'm good at it. It's that it stopped being the point.'

Took first weekend trip using new Friday off: cabin near Mt Hood 2025-11-08 weekend with Sam. Described as 'the good kind of ordinary' — hiking, reading, cooking, no agenda. First Friday off was 2025-11-01.

On 2025-11-22, at Priya's new studio, Priya asked offhand whether Dana was going to go for the director job in the spring like she'd said. Dana said: 'Honestly? I'm not sure I want the director track at all anymore. I'm not sure I ever did.' Dana described having to sit down because 'I think it's true.' Dana said: 'Deferring the promotion, I could file under grief and timing. But "maybe I never wanted it" — that's saying the last fifteen years of climbing might have been momentum, or my mother's voice about not wasting my potential, or just what you do when you're good at something. Not actually mine.' Dana described this realization as 'uncomfortable and correct, which is usually the combination that means something real.' Dana clarified: 'I'm not going to blow up my career over a Tuesday epiphany at a pottery wheel — I have a mortgage and I actually like being competent at the work. But the story where getting to director was the point of me? I don't think I believe it anymore. I think I stopped believing it and only just noticed.' Priya said 'yeah, no kidding' and handed Dana a mug to trim. (2025-11-22)
Priya's new ceramics studio has keys as of November 2025; described as smelling like 'wet clay and fresh paint' (as of 2025-11-22)

entries.note: Serious conflict as of mid-April 2025 over loan request; relationship currently strained with communication paused. See open thread.

Snapped at Sam over dinner question on Tuesday 2025-07-30 ('I genuinely could not care less about dinner, Sam' — cold tone over salmon vs. pasta). Apologized properly next morning; Sam responded graciously ('you're carrying a lot, but I'm not the incident'). Dana described this as uncharacteristic ('six years and we don't do the snipe-at-each-other thing') and attributed it to work stress, not relationship issue. Repaired same-day.

Session 2025-08-16: Dana deferred director promotion offer despite it being 'the rung I've been climbing toward for years' and 'the obvious next move.' Said six months ago would have accepted immediately. Attributed hesitation to 'this year's been a lot' and possibility of 'grief-brain again, same as the flat launch.' Explicitly resisted building theory from single decision: 'I'm not going to build a whole theory out of one deferred promotion. Ask me in the spring.' Pattern of ambition-as-identity meeting sustained ambivalence, but Dana frames as situational/timing rather than identity shift.

Session 2025-10-25: Dana's statement 'it stopped being the point' and choosing four-day week to prioritize barn/ceramics over career advancement represents sustained pattern shift from 'ambition's been my whole spine' and 'the person who never turns down the bigger role.' This is not a single act but a deliberate restructuring of life priorities. However, this appears to be grief-driven transformation rather than contradiction of core identity — the engine is redirecting, not broken.

Work described as 'still a grind' in September; by early October Q4 planning is 'bloodbath' with eleven-hour days. Pattern of work stress leaking into personal relationships emerging (snapped at Sam twice in recent months about work pressure). Dana self-aware: 'not mad at him, mad at the laptop.'

Session 2025-11-08: Dana reports the work-stress interpersonal strain from Q4 planning has fully resolved after moving to four-day week. Weekend at Mt Hood cabin showed 'we were us' again with no tension. Sam observed 'I like you at four days a week already.' Dana described herself as 'lighter' and 'easier to live with' off the work pressure. Strain was situational, not relational.
